Scenario.title: CostaRicto Energy Sector Disruption Campaign
Scenario.overview: Energy Simple A sophisticated spear-phishing campaign targeting energy sector infrastructure leads to exploitation of trusted execution environment vulnerabilities, resulting in operational data manipulation to disrupt critical energy services.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Target System Artifact, OS Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Exploit TEE Vulnerability, Data Manipulation

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker sends targeted phishing emails containing malicious hyperlinks to energy sector employees. These links exploit trust relationships with social networking sites to harvest session cookies and credentials, granting initial access to internal systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-67: String Format Overflow in syslog()
stage.description: Using stolen credentials, the attacker exploits a Trusted Execution Environment (TEE) vulnerability in industrial control systems. This allows execution of arbitrary code through insecure syslog() function implementations, establishing persistence on critical infrastructure.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker manipulates operational data within running OS processes to alter sensor readings and control parameters. This compromises energy grid stability by creating false operational states that disrupt power distribution networks.

Scenario.summary: A multi-stage attack leveraging spear-phishing, TEE exploitation, and data manipulation techniques targets energy sector infrastructure to cause operational disruptions through compromised industrial control systems.
